verbatim_text:
F. No. P -13013/75/2023 -Policy -DD-III 
Government of India  
Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment  
Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan)  
*** 
Pt. Deendayal Antyodaya Bhawan,  
C.G.O. Complex, New Delhi -110003  
Date:   22  September, 2025  
Office Memorandum  
  
Subject:    DEPwD's clarification dated 10.09.2025 on applicability of revised Comprehensive 
Guidelines for conducting competitive written public examinations dated 01.08.2025 for Persons 
with Disabilities under the RPwD Act, 2016.  
  
  
The undersigned is directed to refer to the subject mentioned above and to say that, the 
Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD), vide its OM dated 01.08.2025   had 
revised the written examination Guidelines that w ere earlier issued in 2018 and 2022. Subsequently, vide 
OM dated 10.09.2025 (copy enclosed),  DEPwD issued a clarification that, all competitive public 
examinations notified or to be notified up to and including 31st December, 2025, shall continue to be 
conducted as per the system in vogue prior to the Guidelines dated 01.08.2025. Accordingly, such 
examinations are to be conducted as per the DEPwD Guidelines dated 29.08.2018, the subsequent 
corrigendum, and the Guidelines issued on 10.08.2022. All copies of the Guidelines are available at 
https://depwd.gov.in/en/guidelines -2/. 
  
2.         In view of the above, DoPT and D/o Financial Services are requested to issue suitable instructions 
to the examining bodies/ recruitment agencies under their administrative control to strictly adhere to the 
DEPwD OM dated 10.09.2025 (copy enclosed) for all examinations being conducted up to 31.12.2025.  
   
 (Debala Bhattacharjee)  
Under Secretary to the Government of India  
(email: debala.joarder@gov.in)  
  
To, 
 
1. The Secretary, DoPT  
2. The Secretary, D/o Financial Services

international_law.instrument: UN Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (CRPD)
core_obligations: Access to tertiary and lifelong learning

rpwd_act_2016: Section 16 – Duty of educational institutions to provide inclusive education
Section 17 – Specific measures including teacher training and accessible materials
right_to_education_act_2009: Free and compulsory education for children aged 6–14 including children with disabilities
samagra_shiksha: Support for Children with Special Needs (CwSN)
